Unified Mine Rescue Training (Advanced): Underground Coal and Metal/Nonmetal Mines — IG 115
fields.description: The primary advanced-level curriculum for underground coal and metal/nonmetal mine rescue teams. Covers team organization, exploration and recovery procedures, ventilation control, and firefighting — the core reference most active teams train from.

fields.title: Unified Mine Rescue Training (Advanced) Appendix — Visuals Only
fields.description: Companion visuals book for IG 115 — the diagrams, maps, and illustrations referenced throughout the advanced training guide, without the accompanying text.

fields.title: Initial Mine Rescue Team Training: Coal and Metal/Nonmetal Mines — MSHA 3026 (formerly IG 5)
fields.description: The starting point for a newly formed mine rescue team, coal or metal/nonmetal. Introduces basic team roles, breathing apparatus, and fundamental rescue procedures before moving on to IG 115.

fields.title: Mine Rescue Team Training: Metal and Nonmetal Mines — MSHA 3027 (formerly IG 6)
fields.description: Metal/nonmetal-specific team training, covering procedures and hazards unique to MNM mines that aren't addressed in the coal-focused guides.

fields.title: Advanced Mine Rescue Training: Coal Mines — IG 7
fields.description: Advanced coal-mine-specific rescue training, building on the initial guide with more complex exploration, ventilation, and recovery scenarios found in coal mines.

fields.title: Advanced Skills Training (AST): Activities for Coal Mine Rescue Teams — IG 7a
fields.description: A set of hands-on practice activities and drills for coal mine rescue teams to sharpen the skills covered in IG 7 — useful for regular team practice sessions.

fields.title: Responding to a Mine Emergency: Training Responsible Persons at Underground Coal Mines — IG 110
fields.description: Training aimed at the responsible persons who must recognize and respond to a mine emergency at an underground coal mine before the rescue team arrives — not the rescue team itself.

fields.text: MSHA approved for use with one of the following ______ batteries only: Energizer 522 Duracell MN 1604
Moisture control sponges must be installed _______ when pre-packing the BioPak
If the ______ magnetic wiper is utilized soak both chamois surfaces of the wiper pieces with water
The BioPak breathing chamber has a Tidal Volume greater than ______ liters
BioPak weight fully charged is ______ Pounds
During an alarm test the LED indication should turn to a flashing red with a horn sounding when the pressure gauge reads between ________ psi
Claustrophobia or Anxiety when wearing a SCBA ______  _______ or prevent the use of the BioPak 240 Revolution
The _______ gauge is protected against sudden loss of oxygen in the event of the gauge line severing by a manual disconnect located at the gauge pass through point of the housing
A foreign gas may cause cylinder _______
Always handle oxygen cylinders _______  _______ to prevent damage
Once _________, survivors should never be left alone
________, dry air at sea level is made up of 78 percent nitrogen and 21 percent oxygen
It is generally recommended that teams not ________  __________ foam filled areas
A self-contained breathing apparatus is a completely __________ unit that supplies oxygen or air independently of the surrounding atmosphere
If a team member must return to the fresh air base because of a problem, it is standard practice among teams for the entire team to go back with that _________
When survivors are located, their location, ___________ and condition should be reported immediately to the command center
When looking for survivors, it is ___________ to both look and listen for clues
___________ time should be allowed for a fire area to cool before it is unsealed
__________ air has a specific gravity of one
If the fresh air base is _______, it should be located where it’s assured a fresh air travelway to the surface
________ is a mixture of methane in air that will burn or explode when ignited
Asphyxiates are gases which cause suffocation or ________
The effects of toxic gases depend on the _________, toxicity, and exposure time
If a ______ member must return to the fresh air base because of a problem, it is standard practice among teams for the entire team to go back with that person
________ with specific gravities greater than 1.0 tend to seek low places
__________ should be tested before use following a disaster
Only detectors and chemical analysis can __________ identify a gas
Sulfur dioxide and hydrogen sulfide are ________  _______ gases
The basic principle of mine ventilation is that air always moves from high to low pressure _______

Respiratory arrest is when a life-threatening emergency that occurs when _____ ______stops or when breathing is ineffective, if untreated, it will lead to cardiac arrest, or it can occur at the same time as cardiac arrest
_____ _____is described as when the chest re-expands and comes back up to its normal position after a chest compression
Shock is a life-threatening condition that occurs when the circulatory system can’t maintain adequate______ _____
Give each _______thrust with the intervention of relieving the obstruction, it may be necessary to repeat the thrust several times to clear the airway
_____ _____may cause a range of signs from mild to severe airway obstruction
______ _____is an important tool for identifying whether opioids may be involved in a life-threatening emergency
Too much _____ in the body can overwhelm the brain and depress the natural drive to breathe, this respiratory depression can result in respiratory arrest and cardiac arrest
Whether you are a team member or the Team Leader, there may be times when you need to point out another team member’s________ or inappropriate actions
A successful resuscitation attempt depends on high-quality resuscitation skills, good communication, and effective team _____
The preferred method for opening the airway when you suspect an unresponsive victim has head or neck trauma, is____ ____
When more rescuers arrive on scene you should assign tasks to other rescuers and rotate compressors every ___ -minutes or more frequently if needed to avoid fatigue
A rate of ____ to ____compressions per minute and a depth of at least 2 inches are the rate and depth for chest compressions on an adult
In the START triage system, patients are categorized based on an assessment of respirations, _______, and mental status
An incident __________system is a tool for the command, control, and coordination of resources at the scene of a large-scale emergency involving multiple agencies
The most appropriate care for an open _______injury is to cover the wound with a moist, sterile dressing
Oxygen-enriched atmospheres may cause combustible gas readings to be ____ actual concentrations
The IR (infrared) methane sensor reading is not to be used for methane concentrations below _____ in air
The High Level audio gas alarm consists of ______
The confidence indicator blinks the LEDs once every _____ seconds
How many error events for the instrument are recorded into a FIFO queue in nonvolatile memory and are time stamped?
When the low battery display (shown below) appears, the battery life remaining is ______
If there is a CO2 sensor present in the instrument, it is zeroed _____
During calibration, gas should be applied at a flow rate of _____ lpm
What is the acceptable temperature range for normal operating conditions?
Industrial Scientific recommends that infrequently used lithium-ion batteries be fully charged every _____ months
Air samples taken from behind sealed areas of the mine are analyzed to determine when it’s safe to begin _____ work
When barometric pressure falls, the pressure on the gas is _____
Clean, dry air at sea level is made up of _____ percent nitrogen and _____ percent oxygen
Breathing air containing 5 percent carbon dioxide increases respiration _____ percent, causing difficult breathing
Hydrogen Sulfide is most explosive at _____ percent
04 to 0.05 percent (400 to 500 ppm) is dangerous to life
The explosive range of Acetylene in normal air is _____ percent
This is a mixture of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, methane, oxygen, nitrogen, and hydrogen
_____  is a mixture of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, and air
_____ ventilation is rarely used in mines because the pressure differential is usually not great enough to create a sufficient and steady flow of air through the mine
A “parachute stopping” is considered to be a _____?
_____ are usually built with concrete block walls sealed against the sides and floor, and with some type of airtight roof made of pre-stressed concrete, railroad ties, or steel beams
